title: Function Types, Parameters, Overloads, and Callable APIs · Mastery Check
summary: Refactor status calculations and adapters into callable contracts that preserve inputs, failures, and return relationships. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a callable service transformation toolkit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model parameter, return, callback, this, and side-effect expectations with readable function types.
Reason about callback parameter compatibility under strictFunctionTypes without accepting unsound narrower handlers.
Model optional, default, rest, tuple-rest, and destructured parameters without ambiguous call shapes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Function Type Contracts · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A lookup accepts keys "id" and "status" from records numbered 6–8. Preserve the relationship between the selected key and return type, then reject a callback that handles a narrower record than callers may supply.

Which action demonstrates Function Type Contracts without dropping earlier contracts?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model parameter, return, callback, this, and side-effect expectations with readable function types.
